10.response:
{"Q0":"This game seemed randomized.

So, it seemed possible, to me, an astronaut could leave...
...And then come back.

But, how would I know that?


Although, given what I observed...
...I could, as based on the format of your game, also assume that an astronaut's leaving *may* herald the beginning of the end of the game: once the other astronaut leaves, there's nothing more to dig up—
—right?

And, I don't presume you'd just let us get away with choosing between galaxies for several rounds — irrespective of whether the chance for winning a bonus was random (and, potentially, non-existent)."}

13.response:
{"Q0":"You know I've been trying to get into this game for months, and months, and months now?

Without ever succeeding?


I finally managed to get in, after sooooo long.
But, even then: barely so.



At any rate, do feel free to invite me back for another round of this space journey, if you'll have me."}

0.response:
{"Q0":"For my, specific, session: there was no real need for "strategizing," beyond ensuring my hands *and* arms were positioned in such a way that they wouldn't become fatigued, or hurt.

I could say, that I was anticipating your really trying to mess me up, with the astronaut's leaving.
I found myself actually perplexed, towards trying to recall if a given astronaut's leaving would permanently-impact the rest of my game—
—Or, no."}

7.response: {"Q0":"When the astronaut for a given planet left, I very much felt dis-inclined to click on many greens: as, I could not know what was under them."}

2.response: {"Q0":"I want to say, 47 - 41 out of 100 times."}

12.response: {"Q0":"...I couldn't consistently discern whether the small delays between loading segments were due to my machine, or your assignment."}
